chunk_summary: Structured tele-assessment of infant feeding and complementary feeding errors focuses on identifying technique errors, assessing hydration and growth, and correcting high-impact feeding practices through culturally feasible counseling and follow-ups.
learning_objectives: Understand the core domains of structured tele-assessment for infant feeding, including neonatal history, feeding methods, and red flags.
Identify common complementary feeding errors in India and develop culturally feasible counseling strategies.
Apply decision algorithms to prioritize corrections and escalate cases with persistent growth issues or red flags.
key_facts: Core domains include birth history, feeding method, frequency, latch, output, and red flags like dehydration or respiratory distress.
Common complementary feeding errors in India include late introduction, low energy density porridges, and micronutrient-poor diets.
Structured follow-ups are essential; escalate cases if growth does not improve after two follow-ups or if red flags emerge.
Video-based observation of feeding techniques can rapidly correct errors such as latch and positioning.
Safety requires avoiding 'feeding-only' framing when illness or dehydration is present.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Faltering growth: recognition and management (NICE NG75) emphasizes structured assessment and escalation for organic disease when growth does not improve after follow-ups.
document_key_topics: Decision algorithms for feeding correction and escalation
